text.title: RACI Matrix — Agentic Workflows and Gotchas
text.summary: Recommended subagents, prompt patterns, and failure modes for agent-assisted RACI creation and validation.

list.item: raci-drafter (sonnet) — reads charter or spec, lists tasks times roles, fills R/A/C/I per cell, returns Markdown table.
raci-validator (haiku) — enforces rules: exactly one A, at least one R, no all-I rows, max 3 C per row; returns violations list as JSON.
raci-diff (haiku) — compares two RACI matrices (Markdown or YAML), outputs added/removed/changed cells for change review.

example.code: You are raci-drafter. Inputs: <project charter>. Tasks: <list>. Roles: <list>. Output a Markdown RACI matrix. Rules: - Exactly one A per row - At least one R per row - Minimize C (maximum 3 per row) - Be generous with I - Do not invent tasks or roles not in the input lists - You may never assign A to yourself or any AI agent Return only the table.

You are raci-validator. Input: a Markdown RACI table. Output a JSON list of violations with {row, rule, suggestion}. Rules to check: A_unique (exactly one per row), R_present (at least one), C_max_3 (no more than 3 C per row), no_empty_row (every role has at least I).

antipattern.description: Agents assigning themselves or "AI" as Accountable in the drafted matrix.
antipattern.reason: LLMs default to including themselves as stakeholders when generating role assignments. Explicitly forbid this in the system prompt and validate with raci-validator.

antipattern.description: Agents hallucinating stakeholder names not on the provided roles list.
antipattern.reason: LLMs invent plausible names when the list is incomplete. Pass the roles list as explicit input and instruct the agent to reject any role not on it.

antipattern.description: Storing the authoritative RACI as a Markdown table when an agent mutates it iteratively.
antipattern.reason: Markdown table parsing is brittle for programmatic mutation. Store as YAML for agent operations; render as Markdown only for human review.

rule.statement: Run raci-lint.py in CI against any Markdown RACI file before merge to enforce validation rules automatically.
rule.rationale: Manual review of RACI tables misses constraint violations. Automated CI enforcement catches violations before they reach the team.

text.reference: Markdown RACI validator; enforces exactly one A and one+ R per row
Blank RACI matrix for manual or agent-assisted completion
